title: US States Fight to Stop Massive $110 Billion Media Merger
subtitle: A group of states argues that combining Paramount and Warner Bros. will hurt consumers and reduce competition in Hollywood.

summary.en: A group of twelve US states has filed a lawsuit to block the $110 billion merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery. California Attorney General Rob Bonta leads the coalition, claiming the deal will lead to higher prices for movie tickets and cable bills. While the US Department of Justice previously approved the deal, these states are now asking a judge to stop it. Paramount has rejected the claims, calling the lawsuit fundamentally flawed and promising to defend the merger in court. The outcome remains uncertain as international regulators also review the massive deal.
summary.zh: 由十二個美國州政府組成的聯盟已提起訴訟，試圖阻止派拉蒙與華納兄弟探索之間價值一千一百億美元的合併案。加州檢察長羅伯·邦塔領軍該聯盟，聲稱此交易將導致電影票價與有線電視帳單上漲。儘管美國司法部先前已批准該交易，但這些州政府目前正請求法官予以阻止。派拉蒙駁回了這些指控，稱訴訟存在根本性缺陷，並承諾將在法庭上捍衛合併案。由於國際監管機構也在審查這項大規模交易，最終結果仍充滿不確定性。

paragraphs.en: A group of twelve US states has launched a legal battle to stop the $110 billion merger between two of Hollywood’s biggest companies, Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery. The coalition, led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta, filed a lawsuit this week arguing that the deal would create a media giant with too much power. According to The Guardian, the states involved include Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington.
paragraphs.zh: 由十二個美國州政府組成的聯盟已發起法律戰，試圖阻止好萊塢兩大巨頭派拉蒙（Paramount）與華納兄弟探索（Warner Bros. Discovery）之間價值一千一百億美元的合併案。該聯盟由加州檢察長羅伯·邦塔（Rob Bonta）領軍，於本週提起訴訟，主張這項交易將創造出一個權力過大的媒體巨獸。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，參與此行動的州別包括亞利桑那州、科羅拉多州、康乃狄克州、麻薩諸塞州、明尼蘇達州、內華達州、紐澤西州、新墨西哥州、紐約州、奧勒岡州以及華盛頓州。

paragraphs.en: This legal challenge is a major problem for the entertainment companies, which hope to finish the merger by September 30. If the deal is delayed past that date, Paramount has agreed to pay a large fee to shareholders, which could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars each quarter. As reported by The Verge, the states argue that the new company would control more than a quarter of all major film releases and basic cable programming in the United States. They believe this lack of competition will force movie theaters and television networks to pay higher fees, costs that will eventually be passed on to the public in the form of more expensive tickets and higher cable bills.
paragraphs.zh: 這項法律挑戰對這兩家娛樂公司而言是一大難題，因為他們原定希望在九月三十日前完成合併。若交易延宕至該日期之後，派拉蒙已同意支付股東一筆鉅額費用，這可能導致公司每季損失數億美元。根據科技媒體《The Verge》報導，這些州政府認為，合併後的新公司將控制全美超過四分之一的主要電影發行與基本有線電視節目。他們擔憂缺乏競爭將迫使電影院與電視網支付更高昂的費用，而這些成本最終將轉嫁給大眾，導致票價與有線電視帳單上漲。

paragraphs.en: "The unlawful merger of these two entertainment behemoths would lead to higher prices, lower quality, and less content for film and television," Rob Bonta said in a statement reported by The Guardian. He added that the states are fighting for fair markets, noting that "no one is above the law." The lawsuit focuses on the fear that without competition, these studios will no longer have to offer fair prices to distributors. Currently, if a studio asks for too much money, a distributor can choose to work with a rival instead. The states argue that this option will disappear if the two companies combine.
paragraphs.zh: 「這兩家娛樂巨頭的非法合併，將導致電影與電視的價格上漲、品質下降且內容減少，」羅伯·邦塔在《衛報》引述的一份聲明中表示。他補充說，各州正為公平市場而戰，並強調「法律之前人人平等」。訴訟焦點在於擔憂若失去競爭，這些製片廠將不再需要向發行商提供公平的價格。目前，若製片廠開價過高，發行商可以選擇與競爭對手合作。各州主張，一旦兩家公司合併，這種選擇權將不復存在。

paragraphs.en: This lawsuit comes as a surprise to some, as the US Department of Justice had already approved the merger in June. However, the states are now asking a judge to stop the transaction until a full review can take place. According to BBC Business, the states have threatened to seek a temporary order to force the companies to stop the deal if they do not comply voluntarily. Outside of the United States, the merger is still waiting for approval from regulators in the UK and Europe. The UK culture secretary, Lisa Nandy, has suggested that her government may need to step in to investigate the deal further, which would cause even more delays.
paragraphs.zh: 這場訴訟讓部分人士感到意外，因為美國司法部已於六月批准了該合併案。然而，各州目前正請求法官在完成全面審查前暫停交易。根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，各州已揚言，若公司不主動配合，將尋求法院發布臨時禁制令，強制要求停止交易。在美國境外，該合併案仍等待英國與歐洲監管機構的批准。英國文化大臣麗莎·南迪（Lisa Nandy）暗示，政府可能需要介入以進一步調查此案，這將導致更多延誤。

paragraphs.en: Paramount has strongly disagreed with the lawsuit. In a public statement, the company described the legal challenge as "fundamentally flawed" and "wrong." They argue that the media industry is changing quickly and that traditional television and cinema are struggling against new technology and streaming platforms. From their perspective, becoming a larger company is an economic necessity to survive. Paramount also noted that delaying the merger would harm entertainment workers who have already faced difficult times as the industry changes.
paragraphs.zh: 派拉蒙強烈反對這項訴訟。該公司在公開聲明中將此法律挑戰描述為「根本性缺陷」且「錯誤的」。他們主張媒體產業正迅速變遷，傳統電視與電影正艱難地對抗新科技與串流平台。從他們的角度來看，擴大規模是生存的經濟必要手段。派拉蒙也指出，推遲合併將傷害那些在產業變革中已面臨困境的娛樂業從業人員。

paragraphs.en: There is also political tension surrounding the deal. The Verge reported that the merger would put CNN under the control of David Ellison, the son of billionaire Larry Ellison, who is known to be a close ally of President Donald Trump. This connection has raised questions about whether the deal received special treatment during the government review process. Bonta hinted at these concerns during a press conference, suggesting that antitrust enforcement—the laws used to ensure fair competition—should act as a check against billionaires who try to gain influence with political leaders.
paragraphs.zh: 此案也籠罩在政治緊張氛圍中。《The Verge》報導指出，合併後將使美國有線電視新聞網（CNN）置於大衛·艾利森（David Ellison）的控制之下，他是億萬富翁賴瑞·艾利森（Larry Ellison）之子，而賴瑞·艾利森眾所周知是川普（Donald Trump）總統的親密盟友。這層關係引發了外界質疑，該交易在政府審查過程中是否獲得特殊待遇。邦塔在記者會上暗示了這些疑慮，並表示反壟斷執法——即確保公平競爭的法律——應作為制衡那些試圖透過政治領袖獲取影響力的億萬富翁的手段。

paragraphs.en: Adding to the tension, there were reports that Paramount might move its operations out of California to avoid the state's strict regulations. Bonta told the BBC that he heard these rumors and felt they were an attempt to pressure regulators into allowing the deal. He made it clear that such tactics would not change the state's position. As the case moves to court, both sides are preparing for a long fight. For now, the future of two of Hollywood’s most famous studios remains uncertain as they face this significant hurdle in their attempt to combine.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，有報導稱派拉蒙可能將營運重心遷出加州，以規避該州嚴格的法規，這加劇了緊張局勢。邦塔向英國廣播公司（BBC）表示，他聽聞這些傳言，並認為這是試圖向監管機構施壓以放行交易。他明確表示，這類手段不會改變該州的立場。隨著案件進入司法程序，雙方正準備迎接長期抗戰。目前，這兩家好萊塢最著名製片廠在合併過程中面臨重大阻礙，未來仍充滿不確定性。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To give costs or charges to someone else, usually the final customer.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 轉嫁（成本或費用）給他人。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指將成本轉嫁給消費者，而非「傳遞」物品。文中：costs that will eventually be passed on to the public in the form of more expensive tickets and higher cable bills.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become involved in a difficult situation or argument in order to help or improve it.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指政府介入調查，而非字面上的「走進去」。文中：The UK culture secretary, Lisa Nandy, has suggested that her government may need to step in to investigate the deal further, which would cause even more delays.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A person or thing that stops something from increasing or continuing.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（檢查），這裡作名詞，指制衡權力。文中：Bonta hinted at these concerns during a press conference, suggesting that antitrust enforcement—the laws used to ensure fair competition—should act as a check against billionaires who try to gain influence with political leaders.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Paramount and Warner Bros sued to block $110bn mega merger

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: US state attorneys general file lawsuit in effort to block Paramount merger

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: States make last-ditch effort to stop the Paramount ‘media behemoth’

previousArticles.title: US States File Lawsuit to Block $110 Billion Media Merger
previousArticles.summaryEn: A group of twelve US states has filed a lawsuit to stop the massive merger between Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery. The states, led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta, argue that the deal will hurt competition and lead to higher costs for consumers. While the US Department of Justice previously approved the merger, these states are now asking a court to step in. Paramount has strongly criticized the lawsuit, claiming it is legally wrong and will hurt entertainment workers. The company hopes to finish the deal by September, despite ongoing investigations in the UK and Europe.

quiz.question: What is the primary financial consequence for Paramount if the merger is not completed by September 30?

options.A: They will be forced to move their headquarters out of California.
options.B: They will be required to pay a substantial fee to their shareholders.
options.C: They will lose their approval from the US Department of Justice.
options.D: They will have to pay higher taxes to the twelve states involved in the lawsuit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Paramount 承諾若合併案在 9 月 30 日後仍未完成，將需支付一筆鉅額費用給股東。選項 A 雖然在文中提到，但那是 Paramount 可能採取的策略而非合併延遲的直接財務懲罰。選項 C 錯誤，因為司法部已經批准了該案。選項 D 為錯誤推論，文中並未提到稅務問題。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why are the states concerned that the merger will lead to higher costs for the general public?

options.A: Because the new company plans to increase production costs for all films.
options.B: Because the merger will result in the closure of all independent movie theaters.
options.C: Because the lack of competition will remove the distributors' ability to negotiate lower prices.
options.D: Because the new company will be forced to pay higher taxes to the twelve states.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第二段與第三段，合併後的公司將掌握超過四分之一的市場份額，這會導致競爭減少。第三段明確指出，目前發行商可以透過選擇競爭對手來壓低價格，但若兩大公司合併，發行商將失去這個談判籌碼，最終導致費用轉嫁給消費者。選項 A 與 B 文中未提及，選項 D 則與事實不符。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the word 'behemoths' refer to?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在文中「entertainment behemoths」用來形容 Paramount 和 Warner Bros. Discovery 這兩家巨大的娛樂公司。Behemoth 原意指巨大的怪物，在此處引申為規模龐大且具影響力的企業。選項 B 錯誤，因為該詞描述的是實體而非行為。選項 C 與 D 與該詞的字面意義及上下文完全相反。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central conflict described in the article?

options.A: A disagreement between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery regarding the terms of their merger.
options.B: A political struggle between the US government and the UK regulators over media control.
options.C: A dispute over whether the merger should move its headquarters to a different state.
options.D: A legal challenge by a coalition of states to block a media merger due to concerns over market competition.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。整篇文章的核心在於描述 12 個州組成的聯盟如何透過法律訴訟試圖阻止 Paramount 與 Warner Bros. Discovery 的合併，理由是擔心市場壟斷導致消費者權益受損。選項 A 忽略了州政府的角色；選項 B 僅是背景細節之一；選項 C 只是文中提到的次要爭議，並非文章的主旨。
